        <h1 class="oc-page-title ">2026 Council meeting agendas and minutes</h1>
        
        <p>Agendas and minutes for 2026 Council Meetings will be published on this page.</p>
<p>Pursuant to the <a href="/files/assets/public/v/1/about-council/documents/governancerules-july2020-electionperiodpolicy2023adopted23october2023.pdf" target="_blank" title="GovernanceRules-July2020-ElectionPeriodPolicy2023adopted23October2023.pdf" class="document ext-pdf opens-in-new-tab" re_target="_blank" re_onclick="" aria-describedby="new-window-0"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Governance Rules 2020<span class="file-info">(PDF, 965KB)</span></a>, Council will publish an Agenda incorporating the business to be dealt for each Ordinary and Special meeting at least six (6) days prior to a scheduled meeting. If it is not possible to comply with this requirement any reason, the Chief Executive Officer must ensure delivery and publication of the Agenda as soon as reasonably possible.</p>
<p>As per the Governance Rules 2020 12.2 (5) A copy of the Minutes shall be sent electronically to every Councillor and placed on the Council website no later than seven (7) days after the Meeting to which they relate.</p>
<p>To submit a question before an Ordinary Council Meeting, refer to the <a href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings/Public-Questions-Submissions-and-Registrations" re_target="" target="blank" re_onclick="">public question and submission page</a>.</p>
<h2>2026 Council meeting agendas and minutes </h2>
<h3><strong>24 March 2026</strong></h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="/files/assets/public/v/3/about-council/documents/council-meetings/2026/24-march-2026/council-meeting-agenda-24-march-2026-public-f.pdf" target="_self" title="Council-Meeting-Agenda-24-March-2026.pdf" class="document ext-pdf"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Council Meeting Agenda - 24 March 2026<span class="file-info">(PDF, 18MB)</span></a></li>
<li><a href="/files/assets/public/v/2/about-council/documents/council-meetings/2026/24-march-2026/council-meeting-minutes-24-march-2026final.pdf" target="_self" title="Council-Meeting-Minutes-24-March-2026.pdf" class="document ext-pdf"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Council Meeting Minutes - 24 March 2026<span class="file-info">(PDF, 1MB)</span></a></li>
<li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/live/PFECXCKujhw?si=frynO5ZQ8C0FWc01">Council Meeting Recording - 24 March 2026</a></li>
</ul>
<p><strong><em>A revised agenda for the 24 March 2026 Council Meeting has been issued.<br>
The revised agenda does not include an item relating to the Review of Advisory Committees. <br>
All other items are unchanged from the previous agenda issued on 18 March 2026.</em></strong></p>
<p><strong style="color: rgb(38,85,105); font-family: inherit; font-size: 1.625em;">24 February 2026</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/files/assets/public/v/3/about-council/documents/council-meetings/2026/24-february-2026/council-meeting-agenda-24-february-2026.pdf" target="_self" title="Council-Meeting-Agenda-24-February-2026.pdf" class="document ext-pdf"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Council Meeting Agenda - 24 February 2026<span class="file-info">(PDF, 15MB)</span></a></li>
<li><a href="https://youtube.com/live/mJ_GM4J7Ua8">Council Meeting Recording - 24 February 2026</a></li>
<li><a href="/files/assets/public/v/2/about-council/documents/council-meetings/2026/24-february-2026/council-meeting-minutes-24-february-2026.pdf" target="_self" title="Council-Meeting-Minutes-24-February-2026.pdf" class="document ext-pdf"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Council Meeting Minutes - 24 February 2026<span class="file-info">(PDF, 2MB)</span></a></li>
</ul>
<h3>10 February 2026</h3>

	<p>Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.</p>

    <div class="col-xs-12">
        <h1 class="oc-page-title ">2025 Council meeting agendas and minutes</h1>
        
        
        <p>Agendas and minutes from current and past Council Meetings for 2025 will be published on this page.</p>
<div data-layout="1" data-title-level="2" class="featured-links featured-links-option-1 wide-layout">
<div class="featured-links-body clearfix">
<ul class="clearfix">
<li><a class="featured-link" href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings" target="" style="">Council Meeting Schedule</a></li>
<li><a class="featured-link opens-in-new-tab" href="https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxa9u02oGErTfcDrdeDA9hw/live" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0" style="">Watch our Council meetings live</a></li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
<p>Pursuant to the <a href="/files/assets/public/v/1/about-council/documents/governancerules-july2020-electionperiodpolicy2023adopted23october2023.pdf" target="_blank" title="GovernanceRules-July2020-ElectionPeriodPolicy2023adopted23October2023.pdf" class="document ext-pdf opens-in-new-tab" aria-describedby="new-window-0"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Governance Rules 2020<span class="file-info">(PDF, 965KB)</span></a>, Council will publish an Agenda incorporating the business to be dealt for each Ordinary and Special meeting at least six (6) days prior to a scheduled meeting. If it is not possible to comply with this requirement any reason, the Chief Executive Officer must ensure delivery and publication of the Agenda as soon as reasonably possible.</p>
<p>As per the Governance Rules 2020 12.2 (5) A copy of the Minutes shall be sent electronically to every Councillor and placed on the Council website no later than seven (7) days after the Meeting to which they relate.</p>
<p>To submit a question before an Ordinary Council Meeting, refer to the <a href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings/Public-Questions-Submissions-and-Registrations">public question and submission page</a>.</p>

<p><strong><em>*A revised agenda for the 15 December 2025 Council Meeting has been issued.<br></em></strong><strong style="font-size: 1em;"><em>The revised agenda does not include an item relating to the adoption of the Flag Policy.<br></em></strong><strong style="font-size: 1em;"><em>All other items are unchanged from the previous agenda issued on 9 December 2025.<br></em></strong><strong style="font-size: 1em;"><em>It is intended that the matter of the Flag Policy be considered by Council in the first half of 2026.<br></em></strong><strong style="font-size: 1em;"><em>This is to enable Councillors to have more opportunity to hear and consider the views of the community.</em></strong></p>

<h2>28 April 2025</h2>
<p><em>In light of the recent passing of Cr Gaetano Greco, the 28 April 2025 Council meeting will be brief, with an amended agenda to acknowledge Cr Greco's leadership, dedication and service to the community and the remarkable legacy he leaves behind in making Darebin a better place for all.</em></p>
<p><em>We wish to take this time to honour and remember Cr Greco's immense contributions to our community. <br></em></p>
<p><em>All other agenda items will be postponed to a later date, and the agenda for that meeting will be published in due course.</em></p>
<p><em>Thank you for your understanding during this difficult time. </em></p>

	<h3 class="footer-title">Acknowledgement of Country</h3>
	<p>Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.</p>

							              <!--normalTemplateStart--><div class="grid">

        <h1 class="oc-page-title ">2024 Council meeting agendas and minutes</h1>
        
        
        <p>Agendas and minutes from current and past Council Meetings for 2024 will be published on this page.</p>
<div data-layout="1" data-title-level="2" class="featured-links featured-links-option-1 wide-layout">
<div class="featured-links-body clearfix">
<ul class="clearfix">
<li><a class="featured-link" href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings" target="" style="">Council Meeting Schedule</a></li>
<li><a class="featured-link opens-in-new-tab" href="https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxa9u02oGErTfcDrdeDA9hw/live" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0" style="">Watch our Council meetings live</a></li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
<p>Pursuant to the <a href="/files/assets/public/v/1/about-council/documents/governancerules-july2020-electionperiodpolicy2023adopted23october2023.pdf" target="_blank" title="GovernanceRules-July2020-ElectionPeriodPolicy2023adopted23October2023.pdf" class="document ext-pdf opens-in-new-tab" aria-describedby="new-window-0"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Governance Rules 2020<span class="file-info">(PDF, 965KB)</span></a>, Council will publish an Agenda incorporating the business to be dealt for each Ordinary and Special meeting at least six (6) days prior to a scheduled meeting. If it is not possible to comply with this requirement any reason, the Chief Executive Officer must ensure delivery and publication of the Agenda as soon as reasonably possible.</p>
<p>As per the Governance Rules 2020 12.2 (5) A copy of the Minutes shall be sent electronically to every Councillor and placed on the Council website no later than seven (7) days after the Meeting to which they relate.</p>
<p>To submit a question before an Ordinary Council Meeting, refer to the <a href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings/Public-Questions-Submissions-and-Registrations">public question and submission page</a>.</p>

	<h3 class="footer-title">Acknowledgement of Country</h3>
	<p>Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.</p>

							              <!--normalTemplateStart--><div class="grid">

        <h1 class="oc-page-title ">2023 Council Meeting Agendas and Minutes</h1>
        
        
        <p>Agendas and minutes from current and past Council Meetings.</p>
<div data-layout="1" data-title-level="2" class="featured-links featured-links-option-1 wide-layout">
<div class="featured-links-body clearfix">
<ul class="clearfix">
<li><a class="featured-link" href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings" target="" style="">Council Meeting Schedule</a></li>
<li><a class="featured-link opens-in-new-tab" href="https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxa9u02oGErTfcDrdeDA9hw/live" target="_blank" aria-describedby="new-window-0" style="">Watch our Council meetings live</a></li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
<p>Pursuant to the <a title="GovernanceRules-July2020-ElectionPeriodPolicy2023adopted23October2023.pdf" href="/files/assets/public/v/1/about-council/documents/governancerules-july2020-electionperiodpolicy2023adopted23october2023.pdf" target="_blank" class="document ext-pdf opens-in-new-tab" aria-describedby="new-window-0"><i aria-hidden="true"></i>Governance Rules 2020<span class="file-info">(PDF, 965KB)</span></a> , Council will publish an Agenda incorporating the business to be dealt for each Ordinary and Special meeting at least six (6) days prior to a scheduled meeting. If it is not possible to comply with this requirement any reason, the Chief Executive Officer must ensure delivery and publication of the Agenda as soon as reasonably possible.</p>
<p>As per the Governance Rules 2020 12.2 (5) A copy of the Minutes shall be sent electronically to every Councillor and placed on the Council website no later than seven (7) days after the Meeting to which they relate.</p>
<p>To submit a question before an Ordinary Council Meeting, refer to the <a href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings/Public-Questions-Submissions-and-Registrations" target="_self">public question and submission page</a>.</p>

        <h1 class="oc-page-title ">2022 Council Meeting Agendas and Minutes</h1>
        
        
        <p>Agendas and minutes from current and past Council Meetings.</p>
<p>Pursuant to the Governance Rules 2020, Council will publish an Agenda incorporating the business to be dealt for each Ordinary and Special meeting at least six (6) days prior to a scheduled meeting. If it is not possible to comply with this requirement any reason, the Chief Executive Officer must ensure delivery and publication of the Agenda as soon as reasonably possible.</p>
<p>To submit a question before an Ordinary Council Meeting, refer to the <a href="https://www.darebin.vic.gov.au/About-council/Council-structure-and-performance/Council-and-Committee-Meetings/Council-meetings/Public-Questions-Submissions-and-Registrations" target="_self">Public Question & Submission page</a>.</p>

	<h3 class="footer-title">Acknowledgement of Country</h3>
	<p>Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.</p>

    Builds the API URL with required libraries(places, geometry, marker, geocoding)
    Uses a Promise and callback(google.maps.__ib__) to confirm when the script is ready

    It Avoids Google’s warning about direct script loading
    Improves page load speed by loading the API asynchronously-->

    
        <script>
            (function (root) {

                        },
                        _fire: function () {
                            if (this._ready) return;
                            this._ready = true;
                            var q = this._cbs.splice(0);
                            for (var i = 0; i < q.length; i++) {
                                try { q[i](); } catch (e) { console.error(e); }
                            }
                        }
                    };
                }
            })(window);

            (g => {
                var h, a, k, p = "The Google Maps JavaScript API", c = "google", l = "importLibrary", q = "__ib__",

                var d = b.maps || (b.maps = {}), r = new Set, e = new URLSearchParams,
                    u = () => h || (h = new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
                        a = m.createElement("script");
                        e.set("libraries", [...r] + "");
                        for (k in g) e.set(k.replace(/[A-Z]/g, t => "_" + t[0].toLowerCase()), g[k]);
                        e.set("callback", c + ".maps." + q);

                    }));
                if (d[l]) {

                } else {
                    d[l] = (f, ...n) => (r.add(f), u().then(() => d[l](f, ...n)));

            })({
                key: "AIzaSyCnFcJLKxfmQykaF7kbx63LTQNsloyObH4",

	<h3 class="footer-title">Acknowledgement of Country</h3>
	<p>Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.</p>

Acknowledgement of Country

Darebin City Council acknowledges the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people as the traditional owners and custodians of the land and waters we now call Darebin and affirms that the Wurundjeri Woi-wurrung people have lived on this land for millennia, practising their customs and ceremonies of celebration, initiation and renewal. Council acknowledges that Elders past, present and future leaders are central to the cohesion, intergenerational wellbeing and ongoing self-determination of Aboriginal communities. They have played and continue to play a pivotal role in maintaining and transmitting culture, history and language.
